edges.description: Stabilized A391E dimers bring FGFR3 kinase domains into an activating configuration, increasing ligand-independent receptor phosphorylation.
Activated FGFR3 creates phosphorylated docking sites and adaptor signaling that feeds the FRS2-GRB2-SOS-to-RAS-MAPK cascade.
Activated FGFR3 also engages STAT signaling as a parallel effector branch.
The same activated receptor state is inferred to perturb FGFR3-expressing epidermal keratinocytes in the cutaneous branch of CSAN.
ERK/MAPK signaling in suture osteoblast-lineage cells accelerates the osteogenic program.
STAT signaling is retained as a parallel FGFR3 effector branch that may contribute to suture-cell transcriptional responses.
Excess osteogenic differentiation and matrix mineralization within the suture causes premature bony fusion.
Premature suture fusion manifests clinically as craniosynostosis.
Dysregulated FGFR3 signaling in keratinocytes is inferred to drive the epidermal thickening and pigmentary features of acanthosis nigricans.
Hyperkeratosis and hyperpigmentation manifest clinically as acanthosis nigricans.

nodes.description: Hyperpigmented, velvety thickening of the skin, typically in flexural areas (neck, axillae, groin). This distinguishes CAN from classic Crouzon syndrome and reflects the broader tissue effects of the specific FGFR3 mutation.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: We now report the unexpected observation of a FGFR3 transmembrane domain mutation, Ala391Glu, in three unrelated families with Crouzon syndrome and acanthosis nigricans, a specific skin disorder of hyperkeratosis and hyperpigmentation.

nodes.description: The A391E receptor has increased ligand-independent activation and enhanced phosphorylation efficiency at critical activation-loop tyrosines. This activated receptor state is the branch point for downstream RAS-MAPK, STAT, and cutaneous FGFR3 signaling outputs.

biological_processes: fibroblast growth factor receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: The A391E mutation enhances FGFR3 activation in the absence of ligand.
evidence.snippet: In Figure 5 we see that the ligand independent activation of the mature A391E mutant receptor is higher than the activation of the mature wild-type receptor, over a wide range of receptor concentrations.

evidence.reference_title: Multiple consequences of a single amino acid pathogenic RTK mutation: the A391E mutation in FGFR3.
evidence.snippet: the mutation also facilitates the phosphorylation of critical tyrosines in the activation loop of FGFR3

molecular_functions: transmembrane receptor protein tyrosine kinase activity

nodes.description: In cranial sutures, activated FGFR3 signaling biases osteoblast-lineage cells toward premature osteogenic differentiation and matrix mineralization. This osteogenic acceleration provides the tissue-level bridge from receptor signaling to early suture fusion.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 2 promotes osteogenic differentiation in mesenchymal cells via ERK1/2 and protein kinase C signaling.
evidence.snippet: Both WT and MT FGFR2 increased early and late osteoblast gene expression and matrix mineralization.

nodes.description: Surgical management of craniosynostosis similar to classic Crouzon syndrome.
Premature fusion of cranial sutures, typically multiple sutures including coronal sutures, similar to classic Crouzon syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: Crouzon syndrome, an autosomal dominant condition characterized by craniosynostosis, ocular proptosis and midface hypoplasia, is associated with mutations in fibroblast growth factor receptor 2 (FGFR2)

nodes.description: Topical treatments for acanthosis nigricans, though the skin changes are typically cosmetic rather than medically significant.

The cutaneous outcome of CSAN is a hyperkeratotic, hyperpigmented epidermal change consistent with acanthosis nigricans. This node captures the tissue-level skin change before the clinical phenotype node.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: acanthosis nigricans, a specific skin disorder of hyperkeratosis and hyperpigmentation.
FGFR3 | HGNC:3690 | Crouzon syndrome-acanthosis nigricans syndrome | MONDO:0012833 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Recurrent missense variant c.1172C>A (p.Ala391Glu) in the FGFR3 transmembrane domain.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: We now report the unexpected observation of a FGFR3 transmembrane domain mutation, Ala391Glu, in three unrelated families with Crouzon syndrome and acanthosis nigricans, a specific skin disorder of hyperkeratosis and hyperpigmentation.

evidence.reference_title: The molecular and genetic basis of fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 disorders: the achondroplasia family of skeletal dysplasias, Muenke craniosynostosis, and Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the FGFR3 gene also result in hypochondroplasia, the lethal thanatophoric dysplasias, the recently described SADDAN (severe achondroplasia with developmental delay and acanthosis nigricans) dysplasia, and two craniosynostosis disorders: Muenke coronal craniosynostosis and Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.

nodes.description: The p.Ala391Glu substitution in the FGFR3 transmembrane domain stabilizes receptor dimerization and increases ligand-independent activation propensity. This is the proximal receptor-biophysical event that links the recurrent CSAN allele to downstream FGFR3 signaling.

biological_processes: fibroblast growth factor receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: The A391E mutation enhances FGFR3 activation in the absence of ligand.
evidence.snippet: It has been hypothesized that the mutant glutamic acid stabilizes the dimeric receptor due to hydrogen bonding and enhances its ligand-independent activation.

evidence.reference_title: Multiple consequences of a single amino acid pathogenic RTK mutation: the A391E mutation in FGFR3.
evidence.snippet: It is believed that the mutation increases FGFR3 dimerization, and our results verify this.

nodes.description: Acanthosis nigricans in CSAN is inferred to reflect dysregulated FGFR3 signaling in epidermal keratinocytes. This node is provisional because the human genetics are strong but the exact keratinocyte signaling route from A391E FGFR3 to flexural acanthosis remains incompletely resolved.

biological_processes: fibroblast growth factor receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: The association of non-dwarfing and even non-skeletal conditions with FGFR3 mutations reveals the potential for a wide range of FGFR pleiotropic effects as well as locus heterogeneity in Crouzon syndrome.

nodes.description: Activating FGFR3 mutations can also engage ligand-independent STAT signaling. This branch is represented separately from MAPK/ERK because STAT and ERK are parallel FGFR3 outputs with distinct downstream biology.

biological_processes: cell surface receptor signaling pathway via STAT

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 mutations promote apoptosis but do not alter chondrocyte proliferation in thanatophoric dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: ligand-independent activation of the STAT signaling pathway was demonstrated in cultured TD cells

nodes.description: Activated FGFR3 signals through adaptor-mediated RAS-MAPK output. The receptor phosphorylates tyrosine docking sites and feeds cytosolic adaptor signaling, including the FRS2-GRB2-SOS route to RAS, RAF, MEK, and ERK/MAPK activation.

evidence.reference_title: The Fibroblast Growth Factor signaling pathway.
evidence.snippet: Activated FGFRs phosphorylate specific tyrosine residues that mediate interaction with cytosolic adaptor proteins and the RAS-MAPK, PI3K-AKT, PLCγ, and STAT intracellular signaling pathways.

nodes.description: Surgical advancement of the hypoplastic midface.
Midface hypoplasia with the characteristic Crouzon facial appearance.

Premature osteogenic differentiation in cranial sutures causes early suture fusion, producing the Crouzon-type craniosynostosis, midface hypoplasia, and proptosis seen alongside the acanthosis nigricans of this FGFR3 syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: Crouzon syndrome, an autosomal dominant condition characterized by craniosynostosis, ocular proptosis and midface hypoplasia
